_pricing._note: USD, 8x10 / 11x14 / 16x20 — gives ~70% margin on Gelato base costs

_descriptionTemplate:
An original Calm & Oak {{series}} print — {{descriptor}}. Designed in-house and printed to order on heavyweight 200 gsm Museum-Quality Matte Paper.

Three sizes (8×10, 11×14, 16×20) sized to drop straight into standard frames. Hahnemühle-grade matte fine-art stock. Ships unframed, flat or rolled.

Made for quiet rooms — pairs naturally with our other Japandi prints as a gallery wall set. Visit calmandoak.com for the full collection.

prints.descriptor: an incomplete circle, the brushstroke that says the rest
a single tall grass arching gracefully, one decisive brushstroke for the whole season
tsuru in mid-flight, the Japanese symbol of longevity drawn in one breath
a single mountain peak emerging from soft ink wash — the contemplation piece
three bamboo stalks rising at varied heights — the odd-number rule, in ink
a single sakura branch with open blossoms and one fallen petal — the transience piece
a single koi crossing still water, with two small vermillion accents
a single decisive vertical brushstroke down the page — water as gesture
an empty wabi-sabi tea bowl, tilted ever so slightly — the imperfection rule made visible
one dried stem in a stoneware bud vase — the whole Japandi philosophy in a single object
a single eucalyptus stem rendered as one continuous line
an olive branch as a single arched ink line — the Japandi outdoor signature plant
three pampas plumes in soft pale-beige texture — the autumn piece
a single fan-shaped ginkgo leaf with radiating vein lines — the most-recognised symbol of stillness
three blades of wild grass at varied heights — the smallest botanical, the calmest piece
three moon phases descending the page — full, half, crescent — the patience piece
a single horizon stroke, a distant bird, a soft sun — the departure piece
a lone tree against a warm terracotta horizon — the golden-hour piece
five horizontal bands in cream, grey, sage and honey — the whole palette as a landscape
a luminous full moon over a faint distant hill — the late-evening piece
soft arches in sage, oat and terracotta — the muted moment, drawn as a horizon
Shizuka 静 — the kanji for stillness, the character that translates the Japandi premise directly
Wa 和 — harmony, the character at the heart of every Japanese aesthetic discipline
Ma 間 — the space between, the pause, the interval; the print that earns its empty wall
oak and walnut overlapping, with one terracotta line — the two-woods rule, made abstract
terracotta, oat and honey-brown vertical bands — the warm palette as a study
stone-grey, warm sage and walnut vertical bands — the botanical palette as a study
a small cairn of three stones — the discipline piece
a tetsubin teapot, two cups, a single branch — chadō, the way of tea, as a still life
light through a billowing linen curtain — the slow-morning piece
